---
title: "Getting Set Up"
subtitle: "Choose a tool, pick a plan, install it"
---

## What to expect at the bootcamp

Two lunch sessions, watch-along format. You do not need to install anything beforehand. Bring a laptop if you want to follow along, but it is not required. The main thing to bring is a task you'd like to try — something from your teaching or research where you spend time on mechanical work.

---

## Two ways to use AI tools

| | Chat tools | Agentic tools |
|---|---|---|
| **What they do** | Answer questions, draft text, summarize uploaded files | Work directly on files in your project folders |
| **Interface** | Web browser or desktop app | Terminal (text-based command line) |
| **Setup** | Create an account, start typing | Run a one-line installer in your terminal |
| **Best for starting** | Quick tasks, brainstorming, one-off drafts | Multi-step workflows, code, file management |
| **Examples** | Claude, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot | Claude Code, OpenAI Codex |

**For this bootcamp**, we demo agentic tools because they show a bigger jump in usefulness. But chat tools are a perfectly good place to start.

---

## Claude Code vs. OpenAI Codex

These are the two main agentic tools we use. Both run in your terminal and can read, write, and run files.

| | Claude Code | OpenAI Codex |
|---|---|---|
| **Made by** | Anthropic | OpenAI |
| **Best models** | Claude Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6 | GPT-5.4, o3 |
| **Install** | Native installer (see below) | Binary download or npm (see below) |
| **Launch** | `claude` | `codex` |
| **Sign in with** | Claude account or Anthropic Console | ChatGPT account or OpenAI API key |
| **Simplest plan** | Claude Pro ($20/mo) | ChatGPT Plus ($20/mo) |
| **Heavy use plan** | Claude Max ($100+/mo) or API billing | ChatGPT Pro ($200/mo) or API billing |
| **Rule files** | `CLAUDE.md` | `AGENTS.md` |
| **Integrations** | Gmail, Google Calendar, Todoist, and more via MCP | Fewer built-in integrations currently |
| **Strengths** | Long context, strong writing, extensive tool ecosystem | Strong coding, fast iteration |

Both are good. We primarily demo Claude Code, but the concepts transfer.

---

## Plans and pricing

### Subscription plans (simplest way to start)

| Plan | Cost | What you get |
|---|---|---|
| **Claude Free** | $0 | Chat only, limited usage |
| **Claude Pro** | $20/mo | Chat + Claude Code access, generous limits |
| **Claude Max** | $100–200/mo | Much higher limits for heavy use |
| **ChatGPT Free** | $0 | Chat only, limited models |
| **ChatGPT Plus** | $20/mo | Chat + Codex access, better models |
| **ChatGPT Pro** | $200/mo | Highest limits |
| **UVM Copilot** | Free (UVM account) | Microsoft Copilot Chat — good for Office tasks |

**For most faculty**: Claude Pro or ChatGPT Plus is the right starting point. Pick whichever ecosystem appeals to you.

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple"}
**The $20/mo subscription is not just chat.** Claude Pro includes Claude Code (the terminal tool), and ChatGPT Plus includes Codex. You also get access to integrations with Gmail, Google Calendar, Todoist, and more — all the advanced workflows shown on our [Bells & Whistles](resources.qmd) page. You do not need a separate API account or additional purchases.
:::

### API pricing (pay-as-you-go)

If you outgrow the subscription or want more control, both companies offer API billing where you pay per token. Most people don't need this — the subscription covers it. For current pricing, see [BenchLM's LLM pricing tracker](https://benchlm.ai/llm-pricing).

---

## Install guide

### Step 1: Pick your machine, then your tool

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Mac

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Claude Code

**Open Terminal** (search "Terminal" in Spotlight, or find it in Applications → Utilities), then paste:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
```

After it finishes, close and reopen Terminal, then launch:

```bash
claude
```

It will open a browser window to sign in. Use your Claude account (a [Claude Pro subscription](https://claude.ai/pricing) is the simplest option).

::: {.callout-tip collapse="false"}
#### Alternative: Homebrew

If you already use Homebrew, you can install with `brew install --cask claude-code` instead. Note: Homebrew installs do not auto-update — you'll need to run `brew upgrade claude-code` yourself.
:::

### OpenAI Codex

**Option A — Direct download (no prerequisites)**

1. Go to the [Codex releases page](https://github.com/openai/codex/releases)
2. Download the **macOS** binary for your machine (Apple Silicon or Intel)
3. Move it to a folder in your PATH (e.g., `/usr/local/bin/`)
4. Open Terminal and run: `codex`

**Option B — npm (if you already have Node.js 22+)**

```bash
npm install -g @openai/codex
codex
```

Sign in with your ChatGPT account (a [ChatGPT Plus subscription](https://chatgpt.com/) is the simplest option).

### Chat only (no install)

No installation needed. Just open one of these in your browser:

- **Claude**: [claude.ai](https://claude.ai)
- **ChatGPT**: [chatgpt.com](https://chatgpt.com)
- **UVM Copilot** (free with UVM account): [go.uvm.edu/copilot](https://go.uvm.edu/copilot)

:::

## Windows

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Claude Code

**Step 1: Install Git for Windows** (required)

- Download from [git-scm.com/downloads/win](https://git-scm.com/downloads/win)
- Run the installer — accept all defaults
- Make sure "Add to PATH" is checked

**Step 2: Open PowerShell** (search "PowerShell" in the Start menu — **not** Command Prompt), then paste:

```powershell
irm https://claude.ai/install.ps1 | iex
```

**Step 3:** Close and reopen PowerShell, then launch:

```powershell
claude
```

It will open a browser window to sign in. Use your Claude account (a [Claude Pro subscription](https://claude.ai/pricing) is the simplest option).

::: {.callout-warning appearance="simple"}
**Make sure you're in PowerShell**, not Command Prompt. Your prompt should start with `PS C:\`. If you see just `C:\>`, you're in the wrong shell — search for "PowerShell" in the Start menu.
:::

### OpenAI Codex

**Option A — Direct download (no prerequisites besides Git)**

1. Install [Git for Windows](https://git-scm.com/downloads/win) if you haven't already
2. Go to the [Codex releases page](https://github.com/openai/codex/releases)
3. Download the **Windows** binary
4. Open PowerShell and navigate to where you downloaded it
5. Run: `.\codex.exe`

**Option B — npm (if you already have Node.js 22+)**

```powershell
npm install -g @openai/codex
codex
```

Sign in with your ChatGPT account (a [ChatGPT Plus subscription](https://chatgpt.com/) is the simplest option).

### Chat only (no install)

No installation needed. Just open one of these in your browser:

- **Claude**: [claude.ai](https://claude.ai)
- **ChatGPT**: [chatgpt.com](https://chatgpt.com)
- **UVM Copilot** (free with UVM account): [go.uvm.edu/copilot](https://go.uvm.edu/copilot)

:::

:::

### Step 2: Verify it works

After installing, check that the tool is working:

```bash
claude --version   # Should print a version number
claude             # Launches Claude Code
```

Once inside, type `/help` to see what's available, or just start typing a request.

---

## Troubleshooting

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
### "command not found" or "not recognized"

The installer put the tool somewhere your terminal can't find it.

**Mac fix:**

```bash
echo 'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.zshrc
source ~/.zshrc
```

**Windows fix:** Close and reopen PowerShell. If that doesn't work, check that `~\.local\bin` is in your system PATH (search "Environment Variables" in Settings).
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
### Permission errors on Mac

If you get "permission denied" during install:

```bash
sudo mkdir -p ~/.local/bin
sudo chown -R $(whoami) ~/.local
```

Then re-run the installer.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
### Windows: wrong shell

- `'irm' is not recognized` → You're in Command Prompt, not PowerShell. Open **PowerShell** from the Start menu.
- `'bash' is not recognized` → You ran the Mac installer on Windows. Use the PowerShell command instead.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
### Windows: "requires git-bash"

Claude Code on Windows needs Git for Windows. Download it from [git-scm.com/downloads/win](https://git-scm.com/downloads/win), install with defaults, restart PowerShell, and try again.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
### Network or firewall errors

If you get SSL/TLS errors or "failed to fetch," your network may be blocking the download.

**Try an alternative installer:**

- Mac: `brew install --cask claude-code`
- Windows: `winget install Anthropic.ClaudeCode`

If your institution uses a proxy, ask IT for the proxy URL and set it before installing:

```bash
export HTTP_PROXY=http://your-proxy:8080
export HTTPS_PROXY=http://your-proxy:8080
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
### Sign-in problems

- **"Invalid code" error**: The sign-in code expired. Run `/login` inside Claude Code to try again — complete the browser sign-in quickly.
- **"403 Forbidden"**: Make sure your subscription is active at [claude.ai/settings](https://claude.ai/settings).
- **Browser doesn't open (Windows/WSL)**: Set your browser manually: `export BROWSER="/mnt/c/Program Files/Google/Chrome/Application/chrome.exe"`
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
### Run the built-in diagnostic

Claude Code has a doctor command that checks your setup:

```bash
claude doctor
```

This checks your installation, configuration, and network connectivity.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
### Multiple installations conflicting

If you previously installed via npm and now used the native installer, you may have two versions. Remove the old one:

```bash
npm uninstall -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
```

The native installer (at `~/.local/bin/claude`) is the recommended version — it auto-updates.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
### Still stuck?

- Type `/help` inside Claude Code for built-in help
- File an issue: [github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues)
- Ask Emily or Erkmen — we'll troubleshoot with you at the bootcamp
:::


---

## Checking your usage

If you are using Claude Code with a paid subscription:

- `npx claude-spend` — shows session and weekly limits, extra paid usage
- `npx ccusage@latest` — alternative usage tracker

Usage limits can change over time. Some models are cheaper than others.

---

## Optional extras

- **[Ghostty](https://ghostty.org)** (free) — A modern terminal app. Looks nicer than the default.
- **[VS Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/)** (free) — A code editor. Both Claude Code and Codex have VS Code extensions.
- **[Typora](https://typora.io)** ($15) — A Markdown viewer. AI tools produce `.md` files; Typora makes them look like formatted text immediately.

---

## Key terms

| Term | What we mean |
|------|-------------|
| **Agent** | A tool instance carrying out a bounded task with some autonomy |
| **Model** | The underlying AI system (e.g., Claude Sonnet, GPT-4o) |
| **Session** | One conversation or working thread with the tool |
| **Context window** | The working memory in the current session — when full, performance degrades |
| **Artifact** | A concrete output: file, draft, memo, script, table, or slide |
| **Rule file** | Standing instructions (`CLAUDE.md` or `AGENTS.md`) that load automatically |
| **Token** | The unit AI models process — roughly ¾ of a word |
| **MCP** | Model Context Protocol — how tools connect to external services (email, calendar, etc.) |
